page: Microsoft Azure EC8ads_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ECadsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance includes 307 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include integrated graphics processing units and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads. The combination of dedicated CPU allocation, high memory density, and local storage capacity makes this server suitable for database hosting, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks that require significant memory overhead without the need for hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure EC8ads_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the ECadsv6 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance features 307 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile, characterized by its high memory density relative to compute cores, is designed for memory-intensive applications. The server is suited for workloads such as database management, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications that require a stable allocation of dedicated CPU and memory resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC8ads_v6 is an x86_64 server featuring 8 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.

tagline: An AMD-powered, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated vCPUs, high memory density, and local storage for demanding database workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory with 8.0 GB per core
